steps.title: Identify the limitation behind the caution
steps.content: The researchers studied only Serbian customers from a developing economy and found they prioritize speedy delivery and tracking over package aesthetics. They cautioned against applying this to customers broadly. The reason: their sample had narrow geographic and economic representation.

steps.title: Determine what the caution logically suggests
steps.content:
Choice B directly states what the researchers' caution implies: additional research with participants from countries of varying economic development is needed to determine whether delivery speed and tracking outweigh package design broadly. This explains why generalizing from one country and economic context would be premature. The alternatives either ignore the caution by applying the findings globally (A), misidentify the limitation as Serbian sample size rather than geographic scope (C), or speculate about wealthy customers without calling for further research (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
